subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Find the pdf, mean and variance of the chi-squared distribution with degrees of freedom n.
answer: The density function is $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{2^{-n/2}x^{n/2-1}e^{-x/2}}{\Gamma(n/2)} & x\ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$ The expected value is $E(X) = n$ and the variance is $\mathrm{Var}(X) = 2n$.
explanation: Answer: The density function is $f(x) = \begin{cases} \frac{2^{-n/2}x^{n/2-1}e^{-x/2}}{\Gamma(n/2)} & x\ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$ The expected value is $E(X) = n$ and the variance is $\mathrm{Var}(X) = 2n$.
